SEARCH
Package graph

Class IncAgent

java.lang.Object
graph.BinOpAgent
graph.IncAgent
All Implemented Interfaces:
Agent
public class IncAgent extends BinOpAgent
An event-driven agent that increments values received on its input topic by 1 and publishes the result to an output topic.

Design Patterns and Implementation Details:

This class extends BinOpAgent by adapting a unary increment operation to the binary execution pattern of the superclass. It binds the single subscription topic to both of the superclass's input channels. When a message is received, both input cache variables are updated to the same numeric value, executing the increment lambda strategy (x + 1) and publishing the result to the target output topic.

Instant-Fire Execution Behavior:

Because both internal inputs are bound to the same incoming topic, a single message arrival immediately updates both caches simultaneously, satisfying the execution guard block. This means IncAgent produces output on its very first message receipt, without requiring a second subscription update.

Required Core Framework Components (Internal Dependencies):

  • BinOpAgent - The parent strategy-driven binary operator agent class.
  • TopicManagerSingleton - Directory service that handles the subscription and publication routing under the hood.

Code Integration Example:


 String[] subscriptions = { "source_numbers" };
 String[] publications = { "incremented_numbers" };
 
 // Create and register the increment agent
 IncAgent incAgent = new IncAgent(subscriptions, publications);
 
 // Publish a value to trigger execution
 TopicManagerSingleton.get().getTopic("source_numbers").publish(new Message(10.0));
 // Output topic "incremented_numbers" receives the value 11.0
 
 // Clean up resources when done
 incAgent.close();
See Also:

  • TopicManagerSingleton

  • Constructor Details

    • IncAgent

      public IncAgent(String[] subs, String[] pubs)
      Constructs an IncAgent and registers it with the designated input and output topics. Throws an IllegalArgumentException during initialization if the structural requirements (at least 1 subscription and 1 publication) are not met to prevent silent configuration failures.
      Parameters:
      subs - the subscription topics array (must contain at least 1 valid topic at index 0)
      pubs - the publication topics array (must contain at least 1 valid topic at index 0)
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if subscription or publication arrays are null, empty, or if their primary elements are null.
